programmes.show_description: Tuttle: To get some extra supplies for the local orphanage, Hawkeye and Trapper invent a fictional captain. The deception slowly grows until everyone believes Tuttle to be a real person.
The Ringbanger: Hawkeye and Trapper hit it off with a wounded Colonel, but when they discover his combat zeal is costing lives, they conspire to keep him from returning to the front line.
Sometimes You Hear the Bullet: Hawkeye's old friend appears at the 4077th. He's a soldier working on a book called You Never Hear the Bullet that hits a little too close to home for Hawkeye.
Rumpole à la Carte: Rumpole agrees to defend an unlikeable restaurateur when a diner's meal is served to them with an additional, unwanted ingredient.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
The Real Thing: Henry offers words of advice to Matthew when his girlfriend breaks-up with him because she has feelings for an unknown, older gentleman.
Heavy Date: A young man with no particular direction in life has a breakdown after finding out that the girl he is in love with is pregnant.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Ghost Rider: A lonely female novelist is in love with a legendary spy hero who has been dead for twenty years. She buys his sports car, only to find out that this ghost comes with the car.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

Teleshopping.
Man's Best Friend: Part 1: Jonathan and Mark find a dog and take him over to the orphanage to help cheer up the kids.
Man's Best Friend: Part 2: Alex finally meets the family of his dreams but those dreams are soon shattered when he is forced to abandon the dog he has become attached to. (S4 EP2)

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: Rage: Mr Stark is denied a bank loan; this sends him over the edge, and he shoots his wife and daughter. He hides in Laura's house, convinced that she and Jenny are his wife and daughter.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: Little Lou: A widowed man makes a promise to quit the circus and move to Walnut Grove after his wife dies during childbirth.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: The Wild Boy: Part 1: A circus comes to Walnut Grove, and one of the most popular attractions is 'Wild Boy', a boy in a cage who goes mad when the circus owner torments him. Part 1 of 2.
The Nurse: Mary Ellen's ambition to become a nurse is tested by disappointment and an experience with death.
The Intruders: A rival lumber company threatens to put Walton and Sons out of business, and young Ben Walton picks the worst possible time to leave home.
Personal Call: Assumpta decides to become more assertive but as Kathleen points out, she needs to sort out her own life before telling people what to do.
Judgement Day: The village faces an anxious wait as the hospital's future is decided by an NHS inspector. Will an emergency force Sally to leave her home for the first time in months?
Only Time Will Tell: Two devastating revelations shatter Millie Cairns' world, and she is left to contemplate an uncertain future. Janet is not unimpressed with Dr. McKenna's replacement.
Rumpole for the Prosecution: Rumpole agrees to take a case acting for the prosecution. Will he prove a murder suspect's guilt?
Kim: A wounded five-year-old Korean boy whose parents are lost captures the hearts of the 4077th, particularly Trapper who, after consulting his wife, wants to adopt him.
L.I.P.: Corporal Walker is being sent home, and he wants to marry his Korean girlfriend so she and their baby can return with him. Hawkeye promises to start the paperwork rolling.
The Trial of Henry Blake: Henry's on trial and Hawkeye and Trapper are put under house arrest to prevent them from showing up with evidence exonerating Henry.
Dear Dad... Three: Once more Hawkeye writes home to his father. Hawkeye and Trapper colour the skin of a racist patient who asks them to make sure he gets the 'right type' of blood.
The Sniper: Hawkeye is shot at while on a picnic. He finds Frank and Hot Lips taking target practice and accuses Frank of firing wildly but they soon realise a sniper is on the loose.
Carry On, Hawkeye: A flu epidemic leaves Trapper, Frank and Henry out of action. In this Korea-wide epidemic they can't find anyone to come and help with casualties besides Hawkeye.
The Incubator: Hawkeye and Trapper desperately try to acquire an incubator for the camp. They meet Major Morris, a medical officer with three incubators who refuses to let them have one.
House Of Cards: The team find themselves at the centre of a dangerous rescue mission involving a Russian defector. Can the team identify and defeat the sleeper agents before it's too late?
Split!: All clues point to a notorious foreign agent when members of the secret service are murdered. The only problem is that Steed killed the prime suspect five years ago.
Kim: A wounded five-year-old Korean boy whose parents are lost captures the hearts of the 4077th, particularly Trapper who, after consulting his wife, wants to adopt him.
L.I.P.: Corporal Walker is being sent home, and he wants to marry his Korean girlfriend so she and their baby can return with him. Hawkeye promises to start the paperwork rolling.
The Trial of Henry Blake: Henry's on trial and Hawkeye and Trapper are put under house arrest to prevent them from showing up with evidence exonerating Henry.
Paper Chase: Henry is concerned about Matthew's lack of effort towards his upcoming exams.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
The Gift Of Life: An immoral business tycoon, who shares no love with the world or anybody in it, is given the opportunity to atone for the pollution and poverty he's been inflicting.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Man's Best Friend: Part 1: Jonathan and Mark find a dog and take him over to the orphanage to help cheer up the kids.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

Teleshopping.
Fight For Your Life: A trainer is reminded of his own shady past when a fighter he's been mentoring is asked to take a fall.
The People Next Door: The people of Mission Estates don't know that one of their own, a doctor, is hiding a secret. Now Jonathan and Mark must help him confront his past.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: The Wild Boy: Part 2: Dr McQueen learns of young Matthew's whereabouts and seeks custody of him so that he can use him for his own personal gain. Part 2 of 2.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: The Return Of Nellie: Nellie comes home for a visit and discovers that Nancy is more of a spoilt brat than ever.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: The Empire Builders: The railroad is coming to Walnut Grove, and with it the promise of jobs and economic growth. But that joy turns sour when the townspeople learn the side effects.
The Search: Olivia and two of her children become lost on Walton Mountain, while Grandma keeps Erin and Mary Ellen busy doing chores all day.
The Secret: Young Jim-Bob Walton searches for the truth about his birth and finds it shrouded in mystery.
Lost Sheep: The appearance of a young stranger unsettles some of the older residents of the village. Siobhain gets some surprising news.
Lost Souls: Tom attempts to reunite a 6-year-old girl with her family after she is found abandoned on the steps of the hospital. Meanwhile, Phyllis is convinced that her home is haunted.
Private Lives: Dr Finlay suffers some embarrassment when his mother comes to visit. He also clashes with Dr Napier over her treatment of Annie Kirk, who is suffering from a heart condition.
Rumpole and the Children of the Devil: Rumpole defends the Timson family yet again - but this time in family court. Can Rumpole overturn social services decision to take a girl into care?
Deal Me Out: Radar hits a local who is famous for jumping in front of vehicles for the compensation. Captain Pak arrives at the 4077th for a marathon poker game disguised as a conference.
Hot Lips and Empty Arms: Hot Lips receives a letter from home causing her to reassess her life; she learns her friend has married a doctor and now has a beautiful home, a pool, and children.
Officers Only: In gratitude for Hawkeye and Trapper saving his son, a general donates an officers' club to the 4077th with negative consequences. Klinger pretends to be pregnant.
Jacko feels the Italian way of life is not for him, and he continues to search for the perfect woman.
Gold-digging Paulette tries to talk Elmo into buying a country mansion. Jacko thinks she's going too far.
Jacko meets a new lady, thanks to her budgie.
The Last Of The Cybernauts...??: Steed faces the prospect of a confrontation with old foes when a vengeful double agent seeks revenge against the team.
